Let me cut to the chase: two of the four objectives are off track.
This is the one change that would bring about real accountability.
Before we close, I would like to circle back to the onboarding deliverable.
Let me cut to the chase. I have one number, one risk and one proposal.
What I would like to put forward is one owner, one milestone and one date.
Building on that, my take on this is different, and here is the number behind it.
To circle back to where we started: the deliverable is late, and the owner is now named.
So we leave with one milestone and one owner. I will bring the numbers on Monday.
If we had shipped the deliverable in the first quarter, we would be measuring engagement right now.
If you had joined us a year ago, we would already have cascading goals in both countries.
If I were not leading two regions, I would have finished the diagnostic last month.
If the group had more bandwidth, they would have hit the milestone in June.
Let's cut to the chase. Two of the four objectives are off track. Where do you want to start?
With the onboarding deliverable, because it blocks the other three. If we had shipped it in the first quarter, we would be measuring engagement right now instead of arguing about it.
The cross-functional group tells me they do not have the bandwidth. Six people, two meetings, no progress.

Then bandwidth is not the problem. Nobody owns the date, so nobody is accountable for it. What I would like to put forward is one owner and one milestone: a working onboarding journey by September fifteenth.
The academic directors will push back. They will tell you September is unrealistic, and two of them have been here longer than I have.

Then I want them to push back in the room, not after it. If they were not the people who will run this, I would not have asked for their names on the milestone. Their buy-in is not a nice-to-have. It is the deliverable.
If we had shipped that deliverable in Q1, we would be measuring engagement right now.
Please push back if you think the target is wrong. I would rather hear it now.

Danielle, it is Claire. I am recording this the night before the quarterly review, because I would rather you hear it from me than read it in a dashboard. Two of our four objectives are off track. The onboarding deliverable slipped by six weeks, and the cross-functional group that was supposed to own it has met exactly twice. I am not going to pretend I have the bandwidth to fix this myself. So what I need from you on Monday is not a status report. I need you to tell me which single milestone would actually move the needle this quarter, and what you would put forward to get us there. And Danielle, please push back on me if you think the target itself is wrong. Half of this leadership team agrees with everything I say, and that is not accountability. That is silence with good manners.

Danielle, quick one before Thursday. The agenda is yours: the culture initiative, the onboarding milestone, and your proposal for the diagnostic with the academic team. Three things you should know. First, Claire will ask for numbers in the first two minutes, so cut to the chase and give her the key performance indicator before the story. Second, the academic directors have never once been asked for buy-in on anything, so they are going to test you. Do not sell. Ask. Third, if you had joined us a year ago, we would already have cascading goals across both countries, and this meeting would be a formality. We do not, so it is not. Bring one milestone, one owner and one date. That is what accountability looks like in this room.

If we had shipped the deliverable in the first quarter, we would be measuring engagement right now.
If the goals had cascaded last year, every academic team would have its own OKR today.
If I were not leading two countries, I would have delivered the diagnostic in July.
If the group had more bandwidth, they would have hit the milestone in June.
If someone had owned that deliverable, we would not be having this conversation today.
What I would like to put forward is one owner, one milestone and one date.
Let me cut to the chase: two of the four objectives are off track.
If we had shipped that deliverable in Q1, we would be measuring engagement right now.
Building on that, my take on this is different, and here is the number behind it.
Please push back if you think the target is wrong. I would rather hear it now.
